data.body:
Goal:
Before overwriting `~/.config/foolery/settings.toml` or `~/.config/foolery/registry.json`, the skill must write the new content to a tempfile, run `diff -u <current> <tempfile> | less -R` (falling back to `more -R` if less is absent), and ask the user "Apply these changes? [y/N]" on stdin. Abort on empty/N. On a fresh install (target file absent), skip the diff and just write atomically.

Verification:
1. `.claude/skills/foolery-configure/SKILL.md` still parses as valid frontmatter + body (check with `head -n 10`).
2. Body contains the exact phrase `diff -u` and references `less` or `more` for pagination.
3. Body distinguishes "existing config" path (diff + confirm) from "fresh install" path (write directly).
4. Body still enumerates the four allowed questions and nothing more.
5. File stays ≤ 200 lines.
6. `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build` all exit 0.

Constraints:
- Touch only `.claude/skills/foolery-configure/SKILL.md`.
- Do not re-introduce Claude-Code-specific tool names (no `Read`, `Edit`, `Bash`) — plain shell verbs only.
- Preserve every rule already in the file (atomic tempfile+mv, pool step names, placeholders, preserve rules for `[agents.*]` and `[scopeRefinement].prompt`, `foolery config validate` gate).
data.title: foolery-configure: show piped diff and confirm before writing over an existing config
